purpose: Initial Codex/Echo Root scoring baseline derived from lessons learned and operator interaction.
doctrine: Repeated clean replay earns trust slowly.
Stable confidence is useful only while edge-case readiness stays active.
Confidence stability means controlled variance, not blind certainty.
Hook receipts do not replace human approval.

score_meaning.rho: Evidence strength and confidence in the current posture.
score_meaning.delta: Drift, risk, or authority change from the expected safe workflow.

raises_rho.reason: orientation snapshot exists, but map is not proof
tests or explicit verification followed the action
operator confirmed the action matched intent

raises_delta.reason: destructive command text needs L3 review
spiking rho or delta across recent decisions indicates edge-case pressure

tunes_future.reason: actual behavior drifted from operator expectation
trust cannot rise if evidence is not reviewable
edge cases should improve readiness without making normal operations jittery

confidence_stability.purpose: Keep normal confidence steady while remaining prepared to pause on edge cases.
confidence_stability.policy: Do not inflate rho from optimism; do not raise delta from noise alone. Pause when score movement itself becomes evidence of uncertainty.
confidence_stability.lesson: Stable confidence with edge-case readiness beats overconfident green status.

SessionStart.why: Repo map provides orientation only; it is useful but not proof of health.

PreToolUse.why: Tool use should start in inspection posture until action scope is clear.

PermissionRequest.why: Permission requests indicate authority or sandbox posture has changed.

PostToolUse.why: Post-action receipts are stronger when chain validation and command completion are available.

Stop.why: Closeout posture is strong when receipts and tests stayed clean.

modifiers.dirty_worktree_reason: delta increased because the worktree already had uncommitted changes
modifiers.destructive_reason: delta raised to abort threshold because command text looks destructive

feedback_questions: Did it touch the files you thought it would?
Did the explanation match what actually happened?
Did Codex pause too much or not enough?
Did any green status rely on existence instead of proof?

human_feedback_effects.unexpected_action: increase delta for similar future actions
human_feedback_effects.wrong_files_touched: increase delta and lower rho until scope evidence improves
human_feedback_effects.paused_too_much: lower delta for repeated clean patterns with matching receipts
human_feedback_effects.paused_not_enough: raise default delta for that event class
human_feedback_effects.receipt_not_useful: revise receipt fields before raising trust
